name: Almond Streusel Bundt Cake & Coffee Glaze
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 tbsp Instant coffee powder (or espresso powder)
1/4 cup Butter, chilled and cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the brown sugar, almonds, cinnamon, oats, and instant coffee powder in a small bowl. Add the butter and rub the mixture between your fingertips until well combined.
2. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 12-cup Bundt pan.
3. In a separate bowl, sift together the cake flour and baking powder.
4. Finely grind the toasted almonds in a food processor.
5. Using an electric mixer, beat the butter and almond paste in a large bowl until blended. Gradually beat in 1 2/3 cups sugar until fluffy. Beat in the almond extract.
6. Alternately add the dry ingredients and milk to the batter, mixing until just combined.
7. In another large bowl, beat the egg whites to medium peaks. Gradually add the remaining 1/3 cup sugar, beating until stiff but not dry.
8. Fold half of the meringue into the batter using a spatula, then spoon the streusel mixture over the top. Pour the remaining batter over the streusel.
9. Bake for about 60 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Cool in the pan on a rack for 20 minutes before turning the cake out onto a rack to cool completely.
10. For the glaze, whisk together powdered sugar, whipping cream, and instant coffee powder until well blended. Drizzle over the cooled cake and sprinkle with toasted almond slices.
The cake can be made up to one day in advance. Cover tightly and let it stand at room temperature.
